summary: Drew DeVault provides a progress update on Sway, the i3-compatible Wayland compositor, announcing the 0.14.0 release with tray icon support and various bug fixes. The project has grown to over 24,000 lines of C from 94 contributors. Significant parallel work is happening on wlroots, a new library replacing wlc for Wayland compositor underpinnings, with working DRM and libinput backends. The bounty program has a $1,200 pot with payouts underway, and a new Patreon supports ongoing development.
key_points: Sway 0.14.0 adds tray icon support, clipboard synchronization, nvidia proprietary driver support, and numerous i3 compatibility improvements
The wlroots project is making rapid progress as a replacement for wlc, with working DRM and libinput backends including touchscreen and drawing tablet support
Sway has reached significant scale with 94 contributors, 2,345 commits, and packages in almost every Linux distribution
Financial sustainability efforts include a $1,200 bounty program and a Patreon with 26 patrons
money_quotes: It's i3, but for Wayland, plus it's got some bonus features.
Today Sway has 24,123 lines of C (and 4,489 lines of header files) written by 94 authors across 2,345 commits.
Sway packages are available today in the repos of almost every Linux distribution.
I'm very excited about our pace and direction.
I hate this crappy driver. It works, but don't expect to receive much support for it. Linus said it best.
key_insight: Sway's rapid growth in contributors and distribution adoption, combined with the ambitious wlroots rewrite, signals the Wayland desktop ecosystem maturing into a viable i3 replacement.
